echo (Energy and Commodity Holistic Optimiser)
  • »
  • echo API »
  • echo.echo_builder module
  • View page source

echo.echo_builder module

The echo builder module contains functions and classes used for building an echo model from a dict/json network representation.

class echo.echo_builder.Network(*, name: str = 'default_name', components: dict = {}, edges: dict = {}, objectives: dict = {}, **extra_data: Any)

Bases: echo.echo_models.BaseModel

A class for holding our network dict and making it easier to add nodes/edges in the correct format.

add_edge_between_nodes(node_tuple: tuple, resource: echo.configuration.Units, edge_name: Optional[str] = None)
add_edge_between_ports(node_tuple: tuple, port_tuple: tuple, resource: Optional[echo.configuration.Units] = None, edge_name: Optional[str] = None)

Adds an edge between two specified ports on two specified nodes.

add_node_to_components(n_id: str, n_type: echo.configuration.NodeType, ports: Optional[Any] = None, params: Optional[dict] = None, data: Optional[Any] = None)

Adds an asset (node) to the component dictionary. :param ports: dict of info on ports (names, units, parameters) :param n_id: unique node id :param n_type: node type from NodeType class, so we know what to build in echo :param params: any params that belong to the node (asset) rather than a particular port

Returns

None

add_objective(obj_name: str, obj_type: echo.configuration.TariffType, component: Optional[dict] = None, prices: Optional[Any] = None, charges: Optional[list] = None)
add_port_to_existing_node(n_id: str, port_dict: dict)

Updates the port dict of the node to include the new port

name: Optional[str]
validate_network()
validate_new_edge(edge_name: str, node_tuple: tuple) → None

Checks that edge has unique name and unique node tuple.

static validate_new_port(ports: Union[dict, list]) → None

Checks that each port has at least a unit key

class echo.echo_builder.NetworkSet(description, name='default_name')

Bases: object

A network set is a grouping of separate network pieces that we want to be able to optimise/analyse as a group. The networks in a network set must have the same interval duration and time periods. This class can be used to manage single networks too, if useful.

add_network(network: dict)

Add a single network to the network set.

add_networks_from_list(networks: list)

Add a list of networks to the network set.

export_results_to_df()

Exports optimisation results to a pandas dataframe and returns that dataframe

get_echo_model(network_name: str, df: Optional[pandas.core.frame.DataFrame] = None)

Returns an echo model of a network in the network set

get_echo_optimiser(network_name: str)

Returns the echo optimiser for a network in the network set

optimise_network_set(log_file=None, verbose=False)

Optimises the set of networks defined in the class instance.

to_df(node, port)

for each network segment in netset, gets the value of port on node, if that node and port combination does not exist sets the result to nan. :param node: name of node to look at :param port: port to get value of :return: dataframe with timeseries data

echo.echo_builder.add_flex_ports_to_node(node_obj: echo.echo_models.Node, port_list: list, port_units: list)
echo.echo_builder.append_results(result_dict, network_dict, in_place: bool = False)

Takes dict of results from an echo model, and appends them to the correct places in a network dict.

echo.echo_builder.array_length_check(array, length: int, message, scalar_ok=False)

Checks if an array has the correct length.

echo.echo_builder.check_node_has_only_one_port(node_dict: dict)

Checks that a node has only one port defined

echo.echo_builder.construct_echo_edge(system: echo.echo_models.OptimisationGraph, edge_name: str, edge_dict: dict, node_name_dict: dict)
echo.echo_builder.construct_echo_node(system: echo.echo_models.OptimisationGraph, node_name_dict: dict, node, node_dict: dict, df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame)

Works out which type of echo node to build. Builds it, adds it to the graph, and updates the node_name dict. :param system: Echo Optimisation Graph :param node_name_dict: dict of {uid: node_name} :param node: str, node name :param node_dict: dict of attributes relevant to the node :param df: dataframe containing any static time series data

Returns:

echo.echo_builder.construct_echo_objective(system: echo.echo_models.OptimisationGraph, node_name_dict: dict, objective_dict: dict, verbose: bool = True)

Converts all the objectives defined in an objective set to echo objectives, and returns an echo objective set.

echo.echo_builder.convert_dict_to_echo(netw: dict, df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame, verbose: bool = True)

Converts dict directly to echo optimisation graph.

echo.echo_builder.convert_network_to_echo(netw: echo.echo_builder.Network, df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame, verbose: bool = True)
echo.echo_builder.create_battery_node(node_dict: dict)

Creates an echo battery node from the provided node dict.

echo.echo_builder.create_carbon_aggregation_node(node_dict: dict)
echo.echo_builder.create_demand_tariff(tariff_dict: dict, component_obj: echo.echo_models.Port)

Creates an echo demand tariff from a tariff dictionary

echo.echo_builder.create_ev(node_dict: dict, df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame) → echo.echo_models.Node
echo.echo_builder.create_flex_node(node_dict: dict, units: int) → echo.echo_models.Node

Creates an echo flexible node from the provided node dict. A flexible node is a node with a single flexible port with a specified unit.

echo.echo_builder.create_flex_node_with_emissions(node_dict: dict, units: int)
echo.echo_builder.create_inverter_node(node_dict: dict) → echo.echo_models.Node

Creates an inverter node, which has one AC port, and at least one DC port.

echo.echo_builder.create_load_node(node_dict: dict, unit: int, df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame) → echo.echo_models.Node

Creates a node with a demand (import only) port.

echo.echo_builder.create_solar_node(node_dict: dict, df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame) → echo.echo_models.Node

Creates a node with one electrical generation port.

echo.echo_builder.create_tellegen_node(node_dict: dict, port_unit)

Creates an echo tellegen node from the provided node dict.

echo.echo_builder.extract_objectives(optimiser: echo.echo_optimiser.EchoOptimiser) → dict
echo.echo_builder.extract_results(optimiser: echo.echo_optimiser.EchoOptimiser, node_name_dict: dict, results_key: Optional[dict] = None) → dict

Extracts results from an echo model and returns them in a dict. Results key arg allows user to specify which results they want returned.

echo.echo_builder.get_tariff_component_from_node_port_name(tariff_dict: dict, node_name_dict: dict, system: echo.echo_models.OptimisationGraph)

Retrieves an objective component defined in an objective dict from an echo model and returns it.

echo.echo_builder.process_field(field, df)

Checks if a field points to data in a df or if it contains the data directly.

echo.echo_builder.process_single_network(network_dict: dict, interval_duration: int, time_periods: int, df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame, verbose: bool = True)

Ingests a single network in a networkset, converts it to an echo model, runs the optimiser, appends results to the original network, and returns results.

echo.echo_builder.run_echo_optimiser(echo_graph, objective_set, interval_duration, time_periods, expansion_periods=1, discount_rate=0, optimiser_engine='cplex', opt_display=False, logfile=None, verbose=True)

Runs the echo optimiser on an echo graph with an echo objective set. Returns the optimiser object.
